whatThisIs: Score the Revised Cardiac Risk Index (Lee) for predicting major perioperative cardiac events after non-cardiac surgery. Check the six binary factors that apply to your patient - high-risk surgery type (intraperitoneal, intrathoracic, suprainguinal vascular), ischemic heart disease, congestive heart failure, cerebrovascular disease, insulin-treated diabetes, and a preoperative creatinine >2 mg/dL. Sophie Well totals the points and reports the Lee class with its associated cardiac-event risk band. Source: Lee TH et al. Circulation 1999;100:1043-1049.
whenToUse: Use this during the preoperative cardiac assessment for a patient undergoing elective non-cardiac surgery to quantify their baseline 30-day risk of cardiac death, non-fatal MI, or non-fatal cardiac arrest. RCRI is one of the two scores (with NSQIP-MICA) embedded in the 2014 ACC/AHA perioperative algorithm and gates the decision to obtain functional-capacity assessment or non-invasive cardiac testing. It is calibrated for stable elective surgery, not for emergency or trauma operations, and does not replace clinical judgment about an unstable cardiac patient who needs medical optimization before any incision.
